does: This paper introduces Petri, an open-source framework that automates alignment auditing by leveraging AI agents to generate test environments for evaluating other AI models. The core contribution is a system that can systematically probe model behavior across diverse scenarios without requiring extensive manual test case curation. Petri uses AI agents to dynamically create adversarial contexts, edge cases, and challenging situations that expose potential misalignment or harmful outputs in target models. By automating the test environment generation process, the framework reduces the human effort needed to conduct comprehensive safety evaluations and makes alignment auditing more scalable and reproducible across different model architectures and capabilities.
connects: This work builds on the established tradition of adversarial testing and red-teaming in AI safety, extending automated evaluation approaches pioneered by prior benchmark frameworks. It responds to the challenge that manual alignment auditing is labor-intensive and doesn't scale with rapidly increasing model capabilities, drawing on recent advances in using language models for code and test generation. The framework also incorporates insights from interpretability and mechanistic understanding research, which has emphasized the need for targeted behavioral probes to understand model failure modes and alignment weaknesses.
enables: Petri enables faster iteration cycles for AI safety researchers by providing infrastructure for systematic auditing that can be applied to new models as they emerge. The tool facilitates comparative safety analysis across model families and serves as a foundation for building increasingly sophisticated automated red-teaming pipelines. By open-sourcing the framework, the work enables community-driven expansion of test case libraries and auditing strategies, potentially accelerating the development of better alignment evaluation metrics and supporting the creation of safety benchmarks that remain relevant as model capabilities evolve.
